title: glossary-word-content
description: Schema for validating glossary word markdown files (e.g., en.md, fr.md). These files contain YAML front-matter with the term field and markdown content defining the glossary term.

frontmatter.description: YAML front-matter block enclosed by --- delimiters at the start of the file

term.description: The glossary term in UPPERCASE. May include parenthetical additions for abbreviations, opcodes, or clarifications (e.g., 'SATOSHI (SAT)', 'OP_ROLL (0X7A)', 'HOT WALLET (SOFTWARE)'). Numbers should be written out (e.g., '51 PERCENT ATTACK' not '51%').

content_rules.description: Documentation of markdown content structural requirements. This property describes validation rules that should be enforced by content validators.

definition_paragraph.description: The primary definition paragraph immediately following the YAML front-matter

required.description: A definition paragraph is required immediately after the front-matter

position.description: Must appear directly after the closing --- of the front-matter

guidelines.description: Content guidelines for the definition paragraph
default: Defines what the term is in clear, technical language
For acronyms, start with: Acronym for "[Full Name]".
Should be comprehensive but concise
Use backticks for technical terms: `scriptPubKey`, `SHA256`
Provide deeper technical explanation if needed
Include examples, use cases, or historical context
May include bulleted or numbered lists

code_formatting.description: Rules for inline code and code blocks

inline_code.description: Use single backticks for technical terms: `scriptPubKey`, `SHA256`, `HASH160`

default: `scriptPubKey`
`RIPEMD160`
`OP_CHECKSIG`

math_formulas.description: Rules for mathematical formulas using LaTeX notation

inline_format.description: Single dollar signs for inline math

block_format.description: Double dollar signs for block/display math

default: $$s_A = n_A + t + H(N_A + T \parallel P_A \parallel m_A) \cdot p_A$$

images.description: Rules for including images in glossary definitions

format.description: Standard markdown image syntax with empty alt text

path_pattern.description: Relative path from word directory to assets folder

alternative_names_blockquote.description: Optional blockquote at the END of the file containing alternative names, translations, or etymology

position.description: MUST appear at the very end of the file if present

format.description: Blockquote with arrow symbol and italicized text

pattern.description: Regex pattern: starts with '> ► *' and ends with '*'

content_types.description: Types of content that appear in the alternative names blockquote
default: Alternative English names for the term
French translation or equivalent term
Etymology or origin notes
Related terminology clarifications
> ► *This attack is also known as the "Goldfinger attack".*
> ► *In French, this is translated as "interface de programmation d'applications" or simply "interface de programmation."*
> ► *In English, it is referred to as a "hash function".*
Use descriptive link text, not raw URLs
Prefer linking to primary sources (BIPs, papers, official repos)
External links should point to stable, reliable resources
